title: Introduction to Arrays
subject: DSA
description: Covers array basics including declaration initialization traversal and common operations. Teaches 1D and 2D arrays with hands-on examples for beginners.

title: Array Rotation and Reversal Techniques
subject: DSA
description: Explains left and right rotation of arrays reversal algorithms and in-place manipulation. Focuses on time complexity analysis of each approach.

title: Sliding Window Technique on Arrays
subject: DSA
description: Teaches the sliding window pattern for solving subarray problems including maximum sum subarray and fixed window problems with step-by-step walkthroughs.

title: Two Pointer Approach for Arrays
subject: DSA
description: Covers the two-pointer technique for problems like pair sum sorted array merging and removing duplicates. Explains optimal time and space complexity.

title: Kadane's Algorithm - Maximum Subarray Sum
subject: DSA
description: Detailed walkthrough of Kadane's algorithm for finding the maximum sum contiguous subarray. Includes proof of correctness and edge case handling.

title: Introduction to Linked Lists
subject: DSA
description: Introduces singly linked lists with node structure creation insertion and deletion. Compares linked lists with arrays and discusses memory allocation.

title: Doubly Linked List Operations
subject: DSA
description: Covers doubly linked list creation bidirectional traversal insertion at head tail and middle and deletion. Explains pointer manipulation in detail.

title: Circular Linked List Concepts
subject: DSA
description: Explains circular linked lists with both singly and doubly circular variants. Covers traversal insertion and deletion with real-world use cases like round-robin scheduling.

title: Linked List Reversal Techniques
subject: DSA
description: Teaches iterative and recursive reversal of linked lists. Includes reversing in groups K-reversal and partial reversal with complexity analysis.

title: Detecting and Removing Cycles in Linked Lists
subject: DSA
description: Covers Floyd's cycle detection algorithm for linked lists finding the start of the loop removing the cycle and proving the algorithm mathematically.

title: Introduction to Stacks
subject: DSA
description: Introduces stack data structure with LIFO principle push pop peek operations and implementations using arrays and linked lists for beginners.

title: Stack Applications - Balanced Parentheses and Expression Evaluation
subject: DSA
description: Covers practical stack applications including balanced bracket checking infix to postfix conversion and postfix expression evaluation with examples.

title: Monotonic Stack Problems
subject: DSA
description: Teaches monotonic stack patterns for problems like next greater element stock span problem largest rectangle in histogram with optimal O(n) solutions.

title: Introduction to Queues
subject: DSA
description: Covers queue fundamentals with FIFO principle enqueue dequeue front rear operations and implementations using arrays and circular arrays.

title: Deque and Priority Queue
subject: DSA
description: Explains double-ended queue and priority queue concepts with heaps. Covers applications in sliding window maximum problem and task scheduling.

title: Queue Using Stacks and Stack Using Queues
subject: DSA
description: Teaches how to implement a queue using two stacks and vice versa with amortized complexity analysis and comparison of both approaches.

title: Recursion Basics and Base Cases
subject: DSA
description: Explains recursion fundamentals recursive functions base conditions recursive calls and the call stack. Covers factorial Fibonacci and power examples.

title: Recursion vs Iteration - When to Use What
subject: DSA
description: Compares recursive and iterative solutions in terms of readability space complexity and performance. Uses sorting tree traversal and Fibonacci as examples.

title: Recursion Practice Problems - Backtracking Introduction
subject: DSA
description: Covers recursion-based problems including string permutations subsets and introduces backtracking for constraint satisfaction problems.

title: Binary Tree Introduction and Traversals
subject: DSA
description: Introduces binary tree node structure with inorder preorder and postorder traversals both recursive and iterative. Covers level order traversal using queues.

title: Binary Search Tree - Insertion Search and Deletion
subject: DSA
description: Explains BST properties insertion search and deletion operations with detailed case analysis for deletion including leaf node one-child and two-child scenarios.

title: BST Validation and Balancing Concepts
subject: DSA
description: Covers how to validate a BST using inorder traversal and range checking. Introduces AVL tree rotations and height-balanced BST properties.

title: Height Diameter and LCA in Binary Trees
subject: DSA
description: Teaches how to find height diameter and lowest common ancestor of binary trees with recursive and iterative approaches and complexity analysis.

title: BFS in Graphs - Level Order Graph Traversal
subject: DSA
description: Introduces BFS algorithm for graphs using adjacency list and queue. Covers multi-source BFS shortest path in unweighted graphs and level-by-level exploration.

title: DFS in Graphs - Depth First Search
subject: DSA
description: Explains DFS algorithm recursively and iteratively for graph traversal. Covers visited tracking component counting and DFS tree structure.

title: Shortest Path Algorithms - Dijkstra
subject: DSA
description: Covers Dijkstra's algorithm for single-source shortest path in weighted graphs with priority queue implementation time complexity and limitations.

title: Shortest Path - Bellman Ford and Floyd Warshall
subject: DSA
description: Explains Bellman-Ford for graphs with negative edges and Floyd-Warshall for all-pairs shortest paths with dynamic programming formulation and complexity analysis.

title: Topological Sort and DAG Applications
subject: DSA
description: Teaches topological sorting using DFS-based approach and Kahn's BFS algorithm. Covers applications in build systems course scheduling and dependency resolution.

title: Cycle Detection in Directed and Undirected Graphs
subject: DSA
description: Explains cycle detection techniques using DFS with color marking for directed graphs and union-find or DFS for undirected graphs with practical examples.

title: Minimum Spanning Tree - Prim and Kruskal
subject: DSA
description: Covers Prim's and Kruskal's algorithms for finding MST. Explains Union-Find data structure for Kruskal and greedy selection in Prim with complexity proofs.

title: Bubble Sort and Selection Sort
subject: DSA
description: Explains bubble sort and selection sort algorithms with step-by-step visualization. Covers time O(n2) and space O(1) complexity and stability analysis.

title: Merge Sort Algorithm
subject: DSA
description: In-depth walkthrough of merge sort with divide and conquer approach. Covers merging step recursive split time O(n log n) and space complexity with examples.

title: Quick Sort and Partition Techniques
subject: DSA
description: Teaches QuickSort with Lomuto and Hoare partition schemes. Covers best average and worst case analysis randomized pivot and practical performance tips.

title: Linear Search and Binary Search
subject: DSA
description: Covers linear and binary search algorithms with conditions for binary search sorted arrays iterative and recursive implementations and time complexity comparison.

title: Binary Search on Answer - Advanced Searching
subject: DSA
description: Teaches binary search on the answer space for optimization problems like minimum days peak element and search in rotated sorted array with generalized templates.

title: ER Model - Entity Relationship Diagram Basics
subject: DBMS
description: Covers ER model fundamentals including entities attributes relationships cardinality and participation constraints. Teaches how to design ER diagrams for real-world problems.

title: ER to Relational Schema Conversion
subject: DBMS
description: Explains the systematic conversion of ER diagrams into relational schemas covering strong and weak entities relationships and multivalued attributes.

title: Extended ER Model - Generalization Specialization
subject: DBMS
description: Covers enhanced ER concepts including generalization specialization aggregation and their representation in schemas with examples from university and company databases.

title: SQL Basics - DDL and DML Commands
subject: DBMS
description: Introduces SQL with CREATE ALTER DROP TABLE commands and INSERT UPDATE DELETE SELECT operations. Covers data types constraints and basic query structure.

title: SQL SELECT Queries and Filtering
subject: DBMS
description: Covers SELECT with WHERE LIKE BETWEEN IN IS NULL clauses ORDER BY GROUP BY and HAVING. Teaches writing effective filter queries with practical examples.

title: SQL Joins - Inner Outer Left Right
subject: DBMS
description: Explains INNER JOIN LEFT JOIN RIGHT JOIN FULL OUTER JOIN and CROSS JOIN with Venn diagram visualization and multiple real-world query examples.

title: SQL Aggregation Functions and GROUP BY
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches COUNT SUM AVG MIN MAX aggregate functions with GROUP BY and HAVING clauses. Covers nested aggregations and filtering on grouped results.

title: Advanced SQL - Subqueries and Views
subject: DBMS
description: Covers correlated and non-correlated subqueries inline views materialized views and WITH clause CTEs. Explains when to use subqueries versus joins for performance.

title: SQL Window Functions and Ranking
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches SQL window functions including ROW_NUMBER RANK DENSE_RANK LEAD LAG and PARTITION BY for analytical queries and reporting use cases.

title: Database Normalization - 1NF 2NF 3NF
subject: DBMS
description: Explains normalization concepts with functional dependencies. Covers 1NF 2NF and 3NF with step-by-step examples of decomposing unnormalized tables.

title: BCNF and Higher Normal Forms
subject: DBMS
description: Covers Boyce-Codd Normal Form 4NF and 5NF with examples. Explains when BCNF decomposition may lose dependency preservation and how to handle it.

title: Functional Dependencies and Closure
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches functional dependencies attribute closure canonical cover and minimal cover. Covers Armstrong's axioms and how to find all valid FDs from given dependencies.

title: Database Transactions and ACID Properties
subject: DBMS
description: Introduces database transactions with ACID properties atomicity consistency isolation and durability. Covers COMMIT ROLLBACK SAVEPOINT with banking transfer examples.

title: Concurrency Control - Locks and Protocols
subject: DBMS
description: Explains concurrency problems like dirty read lost update and phantom reads. Covers shared exclusive locks two-phase locking protocol and deadlock handling.

title: Transaction Isolation Levels
subject: DBMS
description: Covers READ UNCOMMITTED READ COMMITTED REPEATABLE READ and SERIALIZABLE isolation levels. Explains which anomalies each level prevents with practical database examples.

title: Database Indexing - Concepts and Types
subject: DBMS
description: Introduces database indexing with dense and sparse indexes ordered indexes and hash indexes. Explains how indexes speed up queries and their overhead on write operations.

title: B-Tree and B+ Tree Indexing
subject: DBMS
description: Covers B-Tree and B+ Tree structure for database indexing with insertion deletion and search operations. Explains why B+ Trees are preferred in RDBMS.

title: Clustered vs Non-Clustered Index and Query Optimization
subject: DBMS
description: Explains clustered and non-clustered indexes composite indexes and covering indexes. Covers query plan analysis EXPLAIN output and when to add or avoid indexes.

title: Hashing in DBMS - Static and Dynamic Hashing
subject: DBMS
description: Covers hashing for file organization including static hashing collision handling linear probing and dynamic hashing with extendible and linear hashing techniques.

title: File Organization in DBMS
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches heap sequential hash and clustered file organization methods. Compares access patterns insertion cost and suitable use cases for each file organization type.

title: Introduction to Operating Systems and System Calls
subject: OS
description: Introduces OS concepts including kernel vs user mode system calls and OS services. Covers types of operating systems and the role of OS in resource management.

title: Process Management and PCB
subject: OS
description: Covers process lifecycle states PCB structure context switching and process creation with fork. Explains inter-process communication mechanisms like pipes and shared memory.

title: Threads vs Processes - Multithreading
subject: OS
description: Explains the difference between processes and threads with shared memory model user-level vs kernel-level threads and advantages of multithreading for performance.

title: Thread Synchronization - Mutex and Semaphores
subject: OS
description: Teaches thread synchronization problems including race conditions critical section mutex locks and semaphores. Covers the producer-consumer and readers-writers problems.

title: CPU Scheduling Algorithms - FCFS SJF Round Robin
subject: OS
description: Covers major CPU scheduling algorithms including FCFS SJF SRTF and Round Robin with Gantt charts waiting time turnaround time calculations and comparison.

title: Priority Scheduling and Multilevel Queue Scheduling
subject: OS
description: Explains priority-based scheduling with preemptive and non-preemptive variants starvation and aging. Covers multilevel queue and multilevel feedback queue scheduling.

title: Deadlock Detection Prevention and Avoidance
subject: OS
description: Covers the four necessary conditions for deadlock detection algorithms resource allocation graph and banker's algorithm for deadlock avoidance with examples.

title: Deadlock Recovery and Handling Strategies
subject: OS
description: Explains deadlock recovery through process termination and resource preemption. Compares all four deadlock handling strategies with trade-offs and practical OS usage.

title: Memory Management - Paging and Segmentation
subject: OS
description: Covers paging mechanism with page tables TLB address translation and segmentation. Explains logical vs physical address space and memory protection techniques.

title: Virtual Memory and Page Replacement Algorithms
subject: OS
description: Teaches virtual memory concepts demand paging page faults and page replacement algorithms including FIFO LRU LFU Optimal with Belady's anomaly and performance analysis.

title: Memory Allocation Strategies - Fixed and Dynamic Partitioning
subject: OS
description: Covers fixed and dynamic memory partitioning strategies first fit best fit and worst fit algorithms internal vs external fragmentation and compaction techniques.

title: Cache Memory and Memory Hierarchy
subject: OS
description: Explains memory hierarchy from registers to cache RAM and disk. Covers cache levels hit ratio locality of reference and impact of cache on program performance.

title: File Systems and Disk Scheduling
subject: OS
description: Covers file system structure directory organization inode FAT NTFS and disk scheduling algorithms FCFS SSTF SCAN C-SCAN with seek time calculations.

title: OSI Model - Seven Layers Explained
subject: CN
description: Explains all seven layers of the OSI model with functions protocols and data units at each layer. Uses real-world analogies and packet flow examples to clarify layer interactions.

title: OSI vs TCP/IP Model Comparison
subject: CN
description: Compares OSI and TCP/IP reference models layer by layer. Covers why TCP/IP is the practical standard and how OSI layers map to TCP/IP layers with protocol examples.

title: Physical and Data Link Layer Protocols
subject: CN
description: Covers physical layer encoding and transmission and data link layer framing error detection CRC MAC addressing and flow control protocols like Stop-and-Wait.

title: TCP/IP Protocol Suite Overview
subject: CN
description: Introduces the TCP/IP protocol stack with Ethernet IP TCP UDP and application layer protocols. Explains how data flows through layers during internet communication.

title: TCP vs UDP - Comparison and Use Cases
subject: CN
description: Explains key differences between TCP and UDP protocols. Covers connection establishment three-way handshake reliability headers and when to choose TCP vs UDP.

title: TCP Congestion Control and Flow Control
subject: CN
description: Teaches TCP flow control using sliding window and congestion control mechanisms including slow start congestion avoidance fast retransmit and fast recovery algorithms.

title: IP Addressing and Subnetting
subject: CN
description: Covers IPv4 addressing classes subnetting with CIDR notation subnet masks network and host identification. Includes practice problems for subnetting and VLSM.

title: IPv6 - Features and Transition Mechanisms
subject: CN
description: Explains IPv6 address format 128-bit addressing header simplification auto-configuration and comparison with IPv4. Covers dual-stack tunneling and NAT64 transition strategies.

title: Routing Algorithms - RIP OSPF and BGP
subject: CN
description: Covers distance vector routing with RIP link state routing with OSPF and path vector BGP. Explains convergence loop prevention metrics and when each protocol is used.

title: Static and Dynamic Routing Explained
subject: CN
description: Explains static routing configuration advantages and use cases versus dynamic routing protocols. Covers routing table structure routing metrics and default route configuration.

title: NAT and IP Masquerading
subject: CN
description: Teaches Network Address Translation with static NAT dynamic NAT and PAT. Covers how NAT enables private IP ranges to access the internet and its impact on routing.

title: DNS - Domain Name System Explained
subject: CN
description: Covers how DNS translates domain names to IP addresses. Explains recursive and iterative resolution DNS record types (A MX CNAME) caching and DNS hierarchy.

title: DNS Security and DNSSEC
subject: CN
description: Explains DNS vulnerabilities like cache poisoning and DNS spoofing. Covers DNSSEC digital signatures zone signing and deployment challenges for secure DNS resolution.

title: HTTP and HTTPS Protocols
subject: CN
description: Covers HTTP request-response cycle methods GET POST PUT DELETE status codes headers and cookies. Explains HTTPS TLS handshake and certificate validation for secure communication.

title: HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 - Modern Web Protocols
subject: CN
description: Explains HTTP/2 features like multiplexing header compression and server push. Covers HTTP/3 built on QUIC protocol and performance improvements for modern web applications.

title: Classes and Objects in OOP
subject: OOP
description: Introduces classes and objects as blueprints and instances. Covers attributes methods constructors and the difference between class and instance variables with examples in Java and C++.

title: Constructors Destructors and this Keyword
subject: OOP
description: Covers default parameterized and copy constructors destructors and the this pointer. Explains constructor chaining and initialization lists with memory management implications.

title: Access Modifiers and Encapsulation
subject: OOP
description: Teaches encapsulation using private protected and public access modifiers. Covers getter and setter methods data hiding and why encapsulation is essential for maintainable code.

title: Static Members and Nested Classes
subject: OOP
description: Explains static variables and static methods shared across all instances. Covers nested classes inner classes and anonymous classes with practical design pattern examples.

title: Single and Multilevel Inheritance
subject: OOP
description: Covers inheritance basics IS-A relationship single inheritance multilevel inheritance and constructor calling order. Explains how derived classes reuse and extend base class behavior.

title: Multiple and Hybrid Inheritance
subject: OOP
description: Explains multiple inheritance with the diamond problem and resolution using virtual inheritance in C++ or interfaces in Java. Covers hybrid inheritance and ambiguity resolution.

title: Method Overriding and super Keyword
subject: OOP
description: Covers method overriding rules covariant return types and the super keyword for calling parent class methods and constructors with practical code examples.

title: Polymorphism - Compile Time and Runtime
subject: OOP
description: Explains compile-time polymorphism through method overloading and operator overloading and runtime polymorphism through method overriding and virtual functions with vtable internals.

title: Abstract Classes and Interfaces
subject: OOP
description: Covers abstract classes with abstract methods and interfaces with multiple implementation. Explains the difference between abstract classes and interfaces and when to use each.

title: Abstraction and Real-World Design Examples
subject: OOP
description: Introduces abstraction as hiding implementation complexity. Uses real-world analogies and design examples to show how abstraction simplifies interaction with complex systems.

title: Design Principles - SOLID in OOP
subject: OOP
description: Covers all five SOLID principles with code examples showing violations and fixes. Teaches how to design maintainable extensible and loosely coupled object-oriented systems.

title: OOP Relationships - Association Aggregation Composition
subject: OOP
description: Explains HAS-A relationships through association aggregation and composition. Covers UML class diagram notation lifecycle dependencies and when to prefer composition over inheritance.

title: Inter-Process Communication - Pipes Signals Sockets
subject: OS
description: Covers IPC mechanisms including unnamed and named pipes signals shared memory message queues and sockets. Explains synchronization challenges in IPC with producer-consumer examples.

title: OS Boot Process and Kernel Architecture
subject: OS
description: Explains the OS boot sequence from BIOS to kernel loading. Covers monolithic vs microkernel architecture system initialization process 0 and kernel space vs user space boundaries.

title: Operator Overloading in OOP
subject: OOP
description: Teaches operator overloading concepts in C++ and its equivalents in other languages. Covers overloading arithmetic comparison and stream operators with rules and restrictions.

title: OOP Design Patterns - Singleton Factory and Observer
subject: OOP
description: Covers three essential design patterns Singleton for single instance control Factory for object creation and Observer for event-driven programming with implementation in Java and Python.

title: Object Cloning and Shallow vs Deep Copy
subject: OOP
description: Explains object cloning mechanisms shallow copy vs deep copy. Covers copy constructors clone method in Java assignment operator overloading and memory implications of each approach.

title: Introduction to Algorithm Analysis and Big-O Notation
subject: Algorithms
description: Covers time and space complexity analysis with Big-O Big-Theta and Big-Omega notations. Teaches how to analyze loops nested loops and recursive calls to determine algorithmic efficiency.

title: Divide and Conquer Strategy
subject: Algorithms
description: Explains the divide-and-conquer paradigm with examples from merge sort quick sort binary search and Strassen matrix multiplication. Derives recurrences and solves them using the Master Theorem.

title: Dynamic Programming - Memoization and Tabulation
subject: Algorithms
description: Introduces dynamic programming with overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure. Covers top-down memoization and bottom-up tabulation using Fibonacci knapsack LCS and matrix chain multiplication.

title: Greedy Algorithms - Activity Selection and Huffman Coding
subject: Algorithms
description: Explains the greedy choice property and how greedy algorithms build optimal solutions step by step. Covers activity selection problem fractional knapsack Huffman encoding and Prim's/Kruskal's MST.

title: Backtracking - N-Queens Sudoku and Subset Sum
subject: Algorithms
description: Covers backtracking as systematic trial-and-error with pruning. Solves N-Queens problem Sudoku solver subset sum and graph coloring. Explains state-space tree construction and constraint propagation.

title: String Algorithms - KMP and Rabin-Karp
subject: Algorithms
description: Covers pattern matching algorithms including naive O(nm) KMP with failure function O(n+m) and Rabin-Karp with rolling hash. Explains preprocessing phase and applications in text search and bioinformatics.

title: Advanced DP - Longest Increasing Subsequence and Edit Distance
subject: Algorithms
description: Covers advanced DP problems including LIS with binary search O(n log n) edit distance with 2D DP table and matrix chain multiplication. Emphasizes recognising DP patterns and state design.

title: Master Theorem and Recurrence Relations
subject: Algorithms
description: Teaches solving divide-and-conquer recurrences using the Master Theorem with all three cases. Covers substitution method and recursion tree method for recurrences not handled by the Master Theorem.

title: Logic Propositional Calculus and Proof Techniques
subject: Mathematics
description: Introduces propositional logic truth tables logical connectives AND OR NOT implications and biconditionals. Covers tautology contradiction logical equivalence and proof techniques including direct proof and proof by contradiction.

title: Set Theory Relations and Functions
subject: Mathematics
description: Covers set notation union intersection complement difference and Cartesian product. Explains relations properties reflexive symmetric transitive and equivalence relations with Venn diagrams and function types.

title: Combinatorics Permutations Combinations and Pigeonhole Principle
subject: Mathematics
description: Explains counting principles permutations with and without repetition combinations binomial theorem and the pigeonhole principle. Covers inclusion-exclusion principle with algorithmic problem-solving examples.

title: Graph Theory for Computer Science
subject: Mathematics
description: Covers graph theory basics including degree sequences Eulerian and Hamiltonian paths planar graphs graph coloring and spanning trees. Focuses on mathematical properties underpinning graph algorithms.

title: Vectors Vector Spaces and Linear Independence
subject: Mathematics
description: Introduces vectors vector addition scalar multiplication dot product and cross product. Covers vector spaces subspaces linear independence span and basis with geometric intuition for machine learning applications.

title: Matrix Operations Determinants and Inverse
subject: Mathematics
description: Covers matrix addition multiplication transpose inverse and determinant calculation. Explains identity matrix properties of matrix operations conditions for invertibility and applications in transformation and ML.

title: Eigenvalues Eigenvectors and PCA
subject: Mathematics
description: Teaches eigenvalue decomposition characteristic polynomial and finding eigenvectors. Covers diagonalization SVD and applications in PCA dimensionality reduction and solving linear systems in machine learning.

title: Probability Theory and Random Variables
subject: Mathematics
description: Covers probability axioms conditional probability Bayes theorem and independence. Introduces discrete and continuous random variables PMF PDF CDF expectation and variance with Binomial Normal and Poisson distributions.

title: Descriptive Statistics and Exploratory Analysis
subject: Mathematics
description: Teaches measures of central tendency mean median mode and measures of spread variance standard deviation IQR. Covers skewness kurtosis box plots and how descriptive stats guide exploratory data analysis.

title: Hypothesis Testing p-values and Confidence Intervals
subject: Mathematics
description: Explains null and alternative hypotheses p-values Type I and Type II errors t-tests chi-squared tests and ANOVA. Covers confidence intervals statistical significance and application in A/B testing and ML model evaluation.

title: Calculus for Machine Learning - Derivatives and Gradient Descent
subject: Mathematics
description: Covers limits derivatives partial derivatives and the chain rule. Explains gradient descent using calculus of partial derivatives learning rate convex surfaces and the mathematical basis of backpropagation in neural networks.

title: Mathematical Induction and Recurrence Relations
subject: Mathematics
description: Teaches mathematical induction strong induction and well-ordering principle for proving algorithmic correctness. Covers setting up and solving recurrence relations with characteristic equations and generating functions.

title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence - Agents and Environments
subject: AI Fundamentals
description: Introduces AI history key concepts intelligent agents environments PEAS framework and types of agents reflex model goal and utility based. Surveys major AI domains including search planning ML vision and NLP.

title: Search Algorithms - BFS DFS A-Star and Heuristics
subject: AI Fundamentals
description: Covers uninformed search BFS DFS UCS and informed search with greedy best-first and A* algorithm. Explains admissible heuristics consistency Manhattan distance and application in pathfinding and puzzle solving.

title: Constraint Satisfaction Problems
subject: AI Fundamentals
description: Explains CSP formulation with variables domains and constraints. Covers backtracking search with forward checking arc consistency AC-3 and heuristics MRV LCV for solving map coloring scheduling and Sudoku.

title: Game Playing - Minimax and Alpha-Beta Pruning
subject: AI Fundamentals
description: Covers adversarial search with minimax algorithm optimal play in zero-sum games and alpha-beta pruning to reduce search space. Explains evaluation functions move ordering and Monte Carlo Tree Search for complex games.

title: Knowledge Representation and Inference
subject: AI Fundamentals
description: Covers propositional and first-order logic for knowledge representation. Explains inference rules resolution unification and forward/backward chaining. Introduces ontologies semantic networks and Bayesian networks.

title: Introduction to Machine Learning for AI
subject: AI Fundamentals
description: Introduces machine learning as a subset of AI covering supervised unsupervised and reinforcement learning paradigms. Surveys key ML algorithms and explains how ML differs from traditional rule-based AI programming.

title: System Design Introduction - Scalability and High Level Design
subject: System Design
description: Introduces system design concepts including horizontal vs vertical scaling load balancing caching replication and sharding. Covers high-level design methodology requirements gathering capacity estimation and component selection.

title: Load Balancing Caching and CDN
subject: System Design
description: Covers load balancing algorithms round-robin least connections and consistent hashing. Explains caching strategies write-through write-back and cache eviction policies LRU LFU. Introduces CDN architecture for static content delivery.

title: Database Design at Scale - SQL vs NoSQL Sharding and Replication
subject: System Design
description: Covers choosing between SQL and NoSQL databases for scale. Explains database sharding strategies horizontal partitioning consistent hashing and replication with leader-follower and multi-leader models for high availability.

title: Designing a URL Shortener - End-to-End System Design
subject: System Design
description: Walks through designing a URL shortening service covering requirements estimation API design database schema base62 encoding caching with Redis and scaling to millions of requests per second.

title: Message Queues and Event-Driven Architecture
subject: System Design
description: Covers message queue concepts producer-consumer pattern and event-driven architecture using Kafka and RabbitMQ. Explains topic partitioning consumer groups delivery guarantees and decoupling microservices at scale.

title: Microservices Architecture and API Gateway
subject: System Design
description: Compares monolithic and microservices architectures with trade-offs. Covers service decomposition inter-service communication REST vs gRPC API gateway patterns service discovery and distributed tracing for observability.

title: HTML5 and CSS3 Fundamentals
subject: Web Development
description: Covers HTML5 semantic elements forms tables media and CSS3 selectors box model flexbox grid animations and responsive design. Teaches building accessible well-structured web pages from scratch.

title: JavaScript Fundamentals for Web Development
subject: Web Development
description: Introduces JavaScript variables data types functions DOM manipulation events promises async-await and fetch API. Covers ES6 features arrow functions destructuring spread operator and modules for modern web development.

title: React.js - Components State and Hooks
subject: Web Development
description: Covers React fundamentals with functional components JSX props state management using useState useEffect and useContext hooks. Explains component lifecycle forms routing with React Router and building SPA applications.

title: Node.js and Express - Backend REST API Development
subject: Web Development
description: Builds RESTful APIs with Node.js and Express covering routing middleware request-response cycle JSON handling authentication with JWT MongoDB integration and deploying Node applications to production.

title: Database Integration - SQL and NoSQL for Web Apps
subject: Web Development
description: Covers integrating PostgreSQL and MongoDB with web applications. Explains ORM with Sequelize and Mongoose schema design connection pooling transactions and optimising database queries for web application performance.

title: Web Security Fundamentals - OWASP Top 10
subject: Web Development
description: Covers OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities including SQL injection XSS CSRF broken authentication insecure direct object references and security misconfiguration. Teaches secure coding practices input validation and security headers.

title: Full Stack Project - MERN Stack Application
subject: Web Development
description: Builds a complete MERN stack application from scratch with React frontend Node/Express backend MongoDB database and JWT authentication. Covers state management API integration deployment and best practices for full stack development.

title: RESTful API Design Principles and Best Practices
subject: Web Development
description: Covers REST constraints resource naming HTTP methods versioning status codes pagination filtering and documentation with OpenAPI Swagger. Teaches designing intuitive maintainable and well-documented APIs for web services.

title: Cloud Computing Fundamentals - IaaS PaaS SaaS
subject: Cloud Computing
description: Introduces cloud computing concepts deployment models public private hybrid and multi-cloud and service models IaaS PaaS SaaS. Covers key benefits scalability elasticity pay-as-you-go and major cloud providers AWS Azure GCP.

title: AWS Core Services - EC2 S3 RDS and VPC
subject: Cloud Computing
description: Covers essential AWS services EC2 instance types S3 storage classes RDS managed databases and VPC networking. Explains IAM roles security groups elastic load balancing and auto scaling for building cloud applications.

title: Containerization with Docker
subject: Cloud Computing
description: Covers Docker concepts images containers Dockerfile build process Docker Compose for multi-container apps volumes networking and pushing images to Docker Hub. Explains how containers solve dependency and environment consistency problems.

title: Kubernetes - Container Orchestration at Scale
subject: Cloud Computing
description: Introduces Kubernetes architecture with pods deployments services ingress configmaps and persistent volumes. Covers kubectl commands cluster setup with minikube rolling updates horizontal pod autoscaling and Helm charts.

title: Serverless Architecture and AWS Lambda
subject: Cloud Computing
description: Explains serverless computing concepts function-as-a-service cold starts event triggers and stateless execution. Builds serverless applications with AWS Lambda API Gateway DynamoDB and S3 triggers with cost and performance analysis.

title: Cloud Storage and Databases - S3 DynamoDB and Cloud SQL
subject: Cloud Computing
description: Covers cloud storage solutions object storage with S3 managed NoSQL with DynamoDB and managed relational databases with Cloud SQL RDS. Explains data consistency replication backup strategies and cost optimisation.

title: Cloud Security Identity and Access Management
subject: Cloud Computing
description: Covers cloud security fundamentals shared responsibility model IAM policies roles and permissions MFA encryption at rest and in transit VPC security groups and cloud compliance frameworks SOC2 ISO27001.

title: Cyber Security Fundamentals - CIA Triad and Threat Landscape
subject: Cyber Security
description: Introduces cybersecurity fundamentals with CIA triad confidentiality integrity availability. Covers types of threats malware phishing ransomware social engineering attack vectors and the role of security in modern IT infrastructure.

title: Network Security - Firewalls VPNs and IDS
subject: Cyber Security
description: Covers network security controls including stateful firewalls packet filtering VPN technologies IPsec and SSL TLS. Explains intrusion detection systems IDS vs IPS honeypots DMZ architecture and network monitoring.

title: Cryptography Fundamentals - Symmetric and Asymmetric Encryption
subject: Cyber Security
description: Covers symmetric encryption AES DES and asymmetric encryption RSA elliptic curve. Explains key exchange Diffie-Hellman digital signatures hash functions SHA-256 and PKI infrastructure with certificate authorities.

title: Ethical Hacking and Penetration Testing Fundamentals
subject: Cyber Security
description: Introduces penetration testing methodology phases reconnaissance scanning exploitation and reporting. Covers tools like Nmap Metasploit Burp Suite and ethical hacking mindset. Explains CVEs vulnerability scoring and responsible disclosure.

title: Web Application Security and SQL Injection
subject: Cyber Security
description: Covers web application attacks SQL injection XSS CSRF broken access control and insecure deserialization. Teaches secure coding practices parameterised queries output encoding and using OWASP ZAP for web application security testing.

title: Security Operations and Incident Response
subject: Cyber Security
description: Covers SOC operations SIEM tools log analysis and threat hunting. Explains incident response lifecycle preparation detection containment eradication and recovery. Covers forensics basics chain of custody and incident reporting.

title: Identity and Access Management and Zero Trust Security
subject: Cyber Security
description: Explains IAM concepts authentication vs authorisation MFA OAuth 2.0 and SAML SSO. Covers Zero Trust security model principle of least privilege role-based access control and PAM privileged access management.

title: Software Development Life Cycle and Agile Methodology
subject: Software Engineering
description: Covers SDLC phases planning requirements design implementation testing and maintenance. Explains Agile manifesto Scrum framework sprints retrospectives kanban and how agile methodologies improve software delivery.

title: Requirements Engineering and Use Case Modeling
subject: Software Engineering
description: Covers functional and non-functional requirements elicitation techniques use case diagrams user stories acceptance criteria and requirements traceability matrix. Teaches writing clear unambiguous requirements for software projects.

title: Software Architecture Patterns - MVC Layered and Event-Driven
subject: Software Engineering
description: Covers major architectural patterns MVC MVP MVVM layered architecture event-driven microservices and repository pattern. Explains when to choose each pattern trade-offs and how architecture decisions affect scalability and maintainability.

title: Software Project Management - Estimation and Risk
subject: Software Engineering
description: Covers project planning effort estimation techniques function points use-case points and COCOMO. Explains risk identification mitigation Gantt charts critical path method and managing scope creep in software projects.

title: Code Quality Clean Code and Code Review Best Practices
subject: Software Engineering
description: Teaches clean code principles meaningful naming functions single responsibility DRY and KISS. Covers code review process technical debt refactoring strategies static analysis tools and establishing coding standards in teams.

title: Version Control with Git - Branching Merging and Workflows
subject: Software Engineering
description: Covers Git fundamentals commits branches merging rebasing and conflict resolution. Explains Gitflow GitHub Flow and trunk-based development workflows pull requests code review process and Git best practices for team collaboration.

title: UML Diagrams for Software Design
subject: Software Engineering
description: Covers essential UML diagram types class diagrams sequence diagrams activity diagrams use case and state machine diagrams. Teaches how to read and draw UML to communicate software design decisions to stakeholders and developers.

title: Introduction to Design Patterns - Creational Structural Behavioral
subject: Design Patterns
description: Introduces the Gang of Four design patterns categorised as Creational Structural and Behavioral. Explains why design patterns exist how they solve recurring design problems and how to recognize pattern opportunities in code.

title: Creational Patterns - Singleton Factory and Builder
subject: Design Patterns
description: Covers Singleton for controlled instantiation Factory Method and Abstract Factory for object creation and Builder for constructing complex objects step by step. Includes Java implementation examples and anti-pattern warnings.

title: Structural Patterns - Adapter Decorator and Facade
subject: Design Patterns
description: Explains structural design patterns Adapter for interface compatibility Decorator for adding behaviour dynamically Facade for simplifying subsystems Proxy for access control and Composite for tree structures.

title: Behavioral Patterns - Observer Strategy and Command
subject: Design Patterns
description: Covers behavioral patterns Observer for event handling Strategy for interchangeable algorithms Command for encapsulating requests Iterator for traversal and Template Method for defining algorithm skeletons with implementation examples.

title: SOLID Principles in Practice
subject: Design Patterns
description: Covers all five SOLID principles Single Responsibility Open-Closed Liskov Substitution Interface Segregation and Dependency Inversion with before-and-after code examples showing how SOLID leads to clean extensible maintainable designs.

title: Design Patterns in Real Projects - Anti-Patterns and Refactoring
subject: Design Patterns
description: Covers recognising and refactoring anti-patterns like God Object Spaghetti Code and Magic Numbers. Shows how to introduce design patterns incrementally into existing codebases with real-world Java and Python refactoring examples.

title: Software Testing Fundamentals - Types and Levels
subject: Testing
description: Covers types of testing unit integration system and acceptance testing. Explains testing levels black-box white-box and grey-box testing manual vs automated testing and the testing pyramid for balancing test effort.

title: Unit Testing with JUnit and Pytest
subject: Testing
description: Teaches unit testing with JUnit 5 for Java and pytest for Python. Covers test fixtures assertions mocking with Mockito/unittest.mock parameterised tests and code coverage measurement with JaCoCo and coverage.py.

title: Test Driven Development - TDD in Practice
subject: Testing
description: Explains TDD red-green-refactor cycle with hands-on examples. Covers writing tests first making them pass minimal implementation refactoring and how TDD improves design forces modularity and provides regression safety nets.

title: Integration and API Testing with Postman and REST Assured
subject: Testing
description: Covers integration testing strategies API testing with Postman collections environment variables and automated test scripts. Introduces REST Assured for Java-based API testing contract testing and testing microservice interactions.

title: Performance Testing with JMeter and Load Testing Concepts
subject: Testing
description: Covers performance testing types load stress spike soak and volume testing with Apache JMeter. Explains thread groups ramp-up requests per second latency throughput bottleneck identification and performance baseline establishment.

title: Test Automation Frameworks and CI Integration
subject: Testing
description: Builds automated test suites with Selenium for UI testing and integrates them into CI pipelines. Covers Page Object Model test reporting with Allure parallel test execution and maintaining test automation for long-lived projects.

title: DevOps Culture and Principles
subject: DevOps
description: Introduces DevOps as a culture of collaboration between development and operations. Covers CALMS framework culture automation lean measurement sharing CI/CD pipeline overview and how DevOps accelerates software delivery.

title: Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment with GitHub Actions
subject: DevOps
description: Builds CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions with workflows jobs steps and runners. Covers automated testing build artifact creation Docker image push to registry and deployment to staging and production environments.

title: Infrastructure as Code with Terraform
subject: DevOps
description: Introduces Terraform HCL syntax providers resources variables modules and state management. Covers provisioning AWS infrastructure VPC EC2 RDS with Terraform remote state in S3 workspace management and Terraform best practices.

title: Configuration Management with Ansible
subject: DevOps
description: Covers Ansible inventory playbooks tasks roles variables templates and handlers. Teaches automating server configuration application deployment and system administration tasks with idempotent Ansible plays for consistent environments.

title: Monitoring and Observability - Prometheus and Grafana
subject: DevOps
description: Sets up Prometheus for metrics collection and Grafana for dashboards. Covers PromQL queries alert rules Node Exporter application instrumentation with client libraries and building SRE dashboards for production observability.

title: Docker and Kubernetes for DevOps Pipelines
subject: DevOps
description: Covers containerising applications with Docker and orchestrating them with Kubernetes in a DevOps context. Explains CI/CD pipeline integration building Docker images in CI deploying to Kubernetes with Helm and managing rolling deployments.

title: Site Reliability Engineering - SLAs SLOs and Error Budgets
subject: DevOps
description: Introduces SRE principles toil reduction SLA SLO SLI error budgets and reliability targets. Covers incident management on-call practices postmortem culture and the balance between shipping features and maintaining reliability.

title: Python for Data Science - Syntax and Data Structures
subject: Python
description: Covers Python essentials for data science including variables data types lists dictionaries tuples sets comprehensions functions and file I/O. Focuses on Python patterns and idioms used in data science and ML workflows.

title: NumPy - Arrays Broadcasting and Vectorised Operations
subject: Python
description: Introduces NumPy ndarray creation indexing slicing broadcasting and vectorised arithmetic. Explains why NumPy is faster than Python lists memory layout and its role as the foundation of all numerical computation in data science.

title: Pandas for Data Manipulation and Cleaning
subject: Python
description: Covers Pandas Series and DataFrame creation reading CSVs indexing filtering groupby merge pivot tables and handling missing values. Teaches essential data wrangling operations needed for preparing data for ML models.

title: Data Visualization with Matplotlib and Seaborn
subject: Python
description: Covers Matplotlib figures axes line bar scatter and histogram charts and Seaborn heatmaps pairplots and boxplots. Teaches creating publication-quality visualizations for exploratory data analysis and communicating insights.

title: Jupyter Notebooks and Reproducible Data Science
subject: Python
description: Introduces Jupyter notebooks for interactive data exploration covering markdown cells magic commands inline plots version control best practices and organising reproducible data science experiments with clear documentation.

title: Python for Feature Engineering and Data Pipelines
subject: Python
description: Teaches practical feature engineering with Pandas and NumPy encoding categoricals creating interaction features normalisation scaling and building reusable preprocessing pipelines with scikit-learn Pipeline and ColumnTransformer.

title: Introduction to Statistics for Data Science
subject: Statistics
description: Covers population vs sample descriptive vs inferential statistics and types of data nominal ordinal interval ratio. Explains mean median mode variance standard deviation and the importance of statistics in data-driven decision making.

title: Probability Distributions for Data Science
subject: Statistics
description: Covers common distributions Bernoulli Binomial Poisson Normal t-distribution chi-squared and F-distribution. Explains when to use each distribution QQ plots and how distributions underpin statistical tests and ML probabilistic models.

title: Statistical Inference and Hypothesis Testing
subject: Statistics
description: Covers sampling distributions Central Limit Theorem confidence intervals one-sample and two-sample t-tests ANOVA chi-squared goodness-of-fit test and p-value interpretation for data science and ML model evaluation.

title: Bayesian Statistics and Bayesian Inference
subject: Statistics
description: Introduces Bayesian thinking prior likelihood posterior and Bayes rule. Covers conjugate priors Bayesian updating credible intervals and comparison with frequentist statistics. Applies Bayesian inference to classification and regression.

title: Correlation Regression and Statistical Modeling
subject: Statistics
description: Covers Pearson and Spearman correlation simple and multiple linear regression ordinary least squares coefficient interpretation R-squared adjusted R-squared and regression assumptions checking with residual analysis.

title: A/B Testing and Experimental Design
subject: Statistics
description: Covers A/B test design sample size calculation power analysis t-test and chi-squared test for results Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons and pitfalls of A/B testing for data-driven product and ML decisions.

title: Machine Learning Fundamentals and Scikit-Learn Workflow
subject: Machine Learning
description: Introduces supervised unsupervised and reinforcement learning with scikit-learn pipeline. Covers train-test split cross-validation preprocessing model selection and evaluation metrics with hands-on classification and regression examples.

title: Linear and Logistic Regression in Depth
subject: Machine Learning
description: Derives linear regression with gradient descent logistic regression with sigmoid loss and regularisation L1 L2. Covers feature scaling multicollinearity assumption checking and implementing both algorithms in Python from scratch.

title: Decision Trees Random Forests and Gradient Boosting
subject: Machine Learning
description: Covers decision tree construction with Gini and entropy Random Forest bagging and feature importance XGBoost LightGBM gradient boosting mechanics and hyperparameter tuning for winning Kaggle-style ML competitions.

title: Support Vector Machines and Kernel Methods
subject: Machine Learning
description: Explains SVM maximum margin classifier soft margin slack variables the kernel trick RBF polynomial and linear kernels. Covers SVM for classification and regression tuning C and gamma parameters with cross-validation.

title: Clustering Algorithms - K-Means DBSCAN and Hierarchical
subject: Machine Learning
description: Covers K-Means with elbow method silhouette score DBSCAN for density-based clustering and agglomerative hierarchical clustering with dendrograms. Explains when to use each and evaluation challenges in unsupervised learning.

title: Model Selection Cross-Validation and Hyperparameter Tuning
subject: Machine Learning
description: Covers k-fold stratified cross-validation leave-one-out GridSearchCV RandomizedSearchCV and Bayesian optimisation. Explains bias-variance tradeoff learning curves validation curves and model selection criteria AIC BIC.

title: Dimensionality Reduction - PCA LDA and t-SNE
subject: Machine Learning
description: Covers PCA via covariance matrix eigen decomposition and SVD explained variance Linear Discriminant Analysis for supervised reduction and t-SNE for high-dimensional visualization. Applies dimensionality reduction to image and text data.

title: ML Pipeline and Feature Engineering Best Practices
subject: Machine Learning
description: Builds production-ready ML pipelines with scikit-learn Pipeline and ColumnTransformer. Covers feature engineering imputation encoding scaling selection and leakage prevention. Discusses reproducibility versioning and pipeline serialisation.

title: Neural Networks and Backpropagation from Scratch
subject: Deep Learning
description: Builds a neural network from scratch using NumPy covering neuron model activation functions ReLU sigmoid softmax forward pass loss computation backpropagation via chain rule and mini-batch gradient descent.

title: PyTorch Fundamentals for Deep Learning
subject: Deep Learning
description: Introduces PyTorch tensors autograd automatic differentiation nn.Module custom Dataset DataLoader training evaluation loops and GPU acceleration. Covers model checkpointing learning rate schedulers and debugging training issues.

title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Image Recognition
subject: Deep Learning
description: Explains CNN architecture convolution layers filters feature maps pooling and fully connected layers. Implements image classifier with ResNet using PyTorch covers data augmentation transfer learning and GradCAM visualization.

title: Recurrent Networks and LSTMs for Sequence Data
subject: Deep Learning
description: Covers RNN architecture vanishing gradient LSTM gates input forget output cell state and GRU. Applies sequence models to time series forecasting text generation and sentiment analysis with PyTorch implementation.

title: Attention Mechanism and Transformer Architecture
subject: Deep Learning
description: Explains self-attention mechanism scaled dot-product attention multi-head attention positional encoding and the full Transformer encoder-decoder architecture. Foundation for understanding BERT GPT and modern large language models.

title: Transfer Learning and Fine-Tuning Pretrained Models
subject: Deep Learning
description: Explains transfer learning concepts feature extraction vs fine-tuning layer freezing and learning rate strategies. Adapts ResNet EfficientNet and ViT pretrained models to custom classification tasks using PyTorch with limited data.

title: Regularisation and Training Best Practices for Deep Learning
subject: Deep Learning
description: Covers overfitting in deep networks and regularisation strategies including Dropout Batch Normalisation Layer Normalisation L2 weight decay learning rate warmup and cosine scheduling for stable and generalisable model training.

title: NLP Fundamentals and Text Preprocessing
subject: NLP
description: Introduces NLP pipeline with tokenisation stemming lemmatisation stop word removal and part-of-speech tagging using NLTK and spaCy. Covers regular expressions and text cleaning techniques for building NLP applications.

title: Text Representation - TF-IDF Word2Vec and Embeddings
subject: NLP
description: Covers Bag-of-Words TF-IDF sparse representations and dense word embeddings with Word2Vec GloVe and FastText. Explains cosine similarity semantic search and how embeddings capture syntactic and semantic word relationships.

title: Text Classification and Sentiment Analysis
subject: NLP
description: Builds end-to-end text classification for sentiment analysis using TF-IDF with Logistic Regression and fine-tuning BERT with HuggingFace. Covers dataset preparation evaluation with precision recall F1 and handling class imbalance.

title: BERT and Transformer-based NLP
subject: NLP
description: Explains BERT pretraining with masked language model and next sentence prediction objectives. Covers fine-tuning BERT for classification NER question answering using HuggingFace Transformers and the Trainer API.

title: Sequence to Sequence Models and Machine Translation
subject: NLP
description: Covers seq2seq encoder-decoder architecture attention mechanism and beam search for machine translation. Explains teacher forcing BLEU score evaluation and modern translation with the Transformer model and mBART.

title: Named Entity Recognition and Information Extraction
subject: NLP
description: Covers NER for extracting persons locations organisations using spaCy CRF models and transformer-based token classification. Explains IOB labelling scheme relation extraction and applications in document intelligence and KG construction.

title: Computer Vision Fundamentals and Image Processing
subject: Computer Vision
description: Introduces image representation as pixel arrays colour spaces BGR RGB HSV image transformations Gaussian blurring Sobel edge detection morphological operations and thresholding using OpenCV for CV preprocessing pipelines.

title: Image Classification with CNNs - End-to-End
subject: Computer Vision
description: Builds complete image classification pipeline with data loading augmentation model training with ResNet fine-tuning and performance evaluation. Covers GradCAM class activation maps and debugging misclassifications.

title: Object Detection - YOLO and Faster RCNN
subject: Computer Vision
description: Covers object detection with bounding box regression anchor boxes IoU non-max suppression Faster RCNN two-stage detector and YOLOv8 one-stage detector. Explains mAP evaluation metric and training custom detectors.

title: Image Segmentation - Semantic and Instance
subject: Computer Vision
description: Covers semantic segmentation with U-Net FCN DeepLab and instance segmentation with Mask RCNN. Explains pixel-wise classification loss functions Dice and cross-entropy and evaluation with mean IoU metric.

title: Vision Transformers - ViT and DINO
subject: Computer Vision
description: Explains Vision Transformer ViT patch embedding positional encoding and self-supervised pretraining with DINO. Compares CNN vs ViT for image tasks covers DeiT efficient training and the shift from convolutions to attention in CV.

title: Video Understanding and Temporal Models
subject: Computer Vision
description: Covers video classification with 3D CNNs two-stream networks and video transformers. Explains optical flow temporal segment networks action recognition benchmarks Kinetics and UCF101 and video object detection challenges.

title: Introduction to Generative AI - GANs VAEs and Diffusion
subject: Generative AI
description: Surveys the generative AI landscape covering GANs VAEs diffusion models and large language models. Explains what makes a model generative the role of probability distributions and real-world applications in text image audio and code generation.

title: Diffusion Models - DDPM and Stable Diffusion
subject: Generative AI
description: Explains forward noising and reverse denoising in DDPMs U-Net denoising architecture noise prediction loss CLIP text conditioning and how Stable Diffusion combines diffusion with latent space compression for high-quality image generation.

title: Prompt Engineering for LLMs and Image Models
subject: Generative AI
description: Covers zero-shot one-shot few-shot chain-of-thought ReAct and self-consistency prompting for LLMs. Explains system prompt design structured output prompting and negative prompting for Stable Diffusion image generation.

title: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
subject: Generative AI
description: Explains RAG architecture with document chunking embedding models vector databases FAISS Chroma retrieval mechanisms and LLM generation. Builds QA systems over private knowledge bases with LangChain and evaluates with RAGAS.

title: Building LLM Applications with LangChain
subject: Generative AI
description: Introduces LangChain framework for building LLM-powered applications. Covers chains agents tools memory retrieval and output parsers. Builds practical chatbots document QA systems and autonomous agents using OpenAI and open-source LLMs.

title: Fine-Tuning LLMs with LoRA and PEFT
subject: Generative AI
description: Covers supervised fine-tuning LLMs on domain-specific data parameter-efficient fine-tuning with LoRA QLoRA and the HuggingFace PEFT library. Fine-tunes LLaMA and Mistral models with 4-bit quantization on consumer hardware.

title: MLOps Introduction - ML in Production Lifecycle
subject: MLOps
description: Introduces MLOps as intersection of ML DevOps and data engineering. Covers the ML production lifecycle challenges of deploying models at scale data versioning model registry CI/CD for ML and the MLOps maturity model.

title: Experiment Tracking with MLflow and Weights and Biases
subject: MLOps
description: Teaches tracking ML experiments with MLflow runs artifacts model registry and W&B sweeps. Covers logging hyperparameters metrics comparing runs and integrating tracking into PyTorch and scikit-learn training scripts.

title: Model Serving with FastAPI and Docker
subject: MLOps
description: Builds ML model serving REST API with FastAPI covering request validation async endpoints and containerisation with Docker. Covers model serialisation pickle ONNX batch vs real-time inference and deployment to cloud platforms.

title: Data and Model Versioning with DVC
subject: MLOps
description: Introduces DVC for versioning large datasets and ML models alongside code. Covers DVC pipelines remote storage S3 GCS stage caching pipeline reproduction and integrating DVC with Git for end-to-end ML reproducibility.

title: ML Pipelines and Workflow Orchestration with Airflow
subject: MLOps
description: Covers orchestrating ML workflows with Apache Airflow DAGs tasks dependencies and sensors. Explains pipeline components feature engineering training retraining triggers and automating the full ML lifecycle in production.

title: Model Monitoring Drift Detection and Retraining
subject: MLOps
description: Covers monitoring ML models in production for data drift concept drift feature drift and performance degradation. Uses Evidently AI for drift detection explains PSI KS-test monitoring dashboards alerting and automated retraining pipelines.
